_note: Curated real-world market-context artefacts modelled on how large asset managers (BlackRock, J.P. Morgan AM, PIMCO, Fidelity, State Street) publish ad-hoc updates alongside fund facts. URLs point to public landing pages; summaries are curated demo excerpts, not verbatim quotes. Live fetch can replace these at runtime (see market_context_sources.fetch_live).

sources.title: Weekly market commentary: staying selective as volatility returns
sources.summary: We stay modestly pro-risk but selective as a growth scare and repricing of the rate path lift volatility. We favour quality equities and AI beneficiaries, and lean into investment-grade credit over high yield.

sources.title: Guide to the Markets — Q1 2026 edition
sources.summary: Equity valuations remain above long-run averages while the rate path stays data-dependent. Diversification and duration are highlighted as ballast against equity drawdowns.

sources.title: Cyclical Outlook webcast: the case for high-quality bonds
sources.summary: PIMCO argues that starting yields make high-quality fixed income attractive, with duration offering resilience if growth slows. Credit selection matters as spreads are tight.

sources.title: Market alert: what a volatility spike means for long-term plans
sources.summary: A reminder that short-term volatility is normal and that staying invested through drawdowns has historically rewarded diversified, long-horizon investors.

sources.title: iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) — fund overview
sources.summary: Low-cost core exposure to large-cap U.S. equities. Positioned as a portfolio building block for the equity growth sleeve.

sources.title: Wholesaler note: positioning conversations for the quarter
sources.summary: Talking points for advisors: reaffirm strategic asset allocation, use volatility to rebalance toward target weights, and revisit fixed-income duration given the rate outlook.

sources.title: 2026 Global Outlook: investing in a transformed world
sources.summary: Structural forces — AI build-out, shifting supply chains, and higher-for-longer rates — reshape return drivers. Favours being selective within equities and up-in-quality in credit.

sources.title: Earnings season recap: resilient margins, cautious guidance
sources.summary: Corporate margins held up better than feared while forward guidance turned cautious, supporting a quality bias in equity selection.

sources.title: Rates commentary: the path lower is bumpy
sources.summary: Disinflation progress is uneven; the rate-cut path is likely gradual. Favours locking in yields and extending duration selectively.

sources.title: Sustainable investing: integrating ESG into core allocations
sources.summary: How ESG-aware building blocks can sit alongside conventional core exposures without sacrificing diversification, relevant for values-aligned mandates.

sources.title: Mid-year webcast: recalibrating risk after the volatility spike
sources.summary: Following the Q1 volatility episode, the discussion focuses on re-risking selectively, the durability of the AI theme, and the role of bonds as ballast.

sources.title: Monthly chart pack alert: sector leadership broadens
sources.summary: Market breadth improved as leadership broadened beyond mega-cap technology, supporting diversified equity positioning.

sources.title: Market Update: Energy sector implications of a Middle East supply disruption
sources.summary: A geopolitical supply disruption in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and lifted energy equities relative to the broader market. Portfolio energy exposure is via diversified global producers; positioning aligns with the long-term thesis and no fund mandate changes are warranted.
key_points: Oil prices rose sharply on Middle East supply-disruption risk.
Energy equities outperformed the broader market during the episode.
Portfolio exposure is via diversified global energy producers (IXC).
Refiners may face margin pressure from higher crude input costs.
Current positioning aligns with the long-term investment thesis; no mandate changes.
sources.advisor_talking_point: While short-term volatility may increase, the portfolio remains positioned to benefit from higher commodity pricing through its exposure to global energy producers. This is context, not a recommendation to trade.

sources.title: Special market bulletin webcast: oil, geopolitics, and portfolio ballast
sources.summary: An advisor-facing call covering the oil-price move, the role of energy and gold as diversifiers during geopolitical stress, and how to frame the episode for clients without overreacting.
key_points: Energy and gold historically provide ballast during geopolitical stress.
Reaffirm the plan; avoid reactive trading into a volatility spike.
Revisit rebalancing only if allocations drift beyond target bands.
sources.advisor_talking_point: Diversification across energy and gold is doing its job as ballast; our base case is unchanged and we are watching, not reacting.

sources.title: Portfolio manager note: energy exposure and the supply-shock thesis
sources.summary: The fund's energy sleeve is concentrated in diversified global integrated producers that benefit from higher crude pricing; refiners face margin pressure. The manager is not changing positioning — the exposure aligns with the long-term thesis and the fund mandate is unchanged.
key_points: Energy exposure is via diversified global integrated producers (IXC).
Higher crude pricing supports producer earnings; refiners may see margin pressure.
No change to positioning; exposure aligns with the long-term thesis.
No material change to the fund mandate.
sources.advisor_talking_point: The manager views the current energy positioning as consistent with the long-term thesis and is not making changes in response to the event.
